name: Danielle
shortDesc: Manual contract redlining is slow, expensive, and the bottleneck nobody on a legal team actually enjoys. Danielle turns it into an email round-trip: CC the assistant on a contract and get a marked-up draft back in minutes.

hero.title: Contract review that takes minutes,
paragraphs: Reviewing contracts is one of those jobs that's important, repetitive, and disproportionately expensive. In-house counsel and legal-ops teams burn hours per agreement turning NDAs, MSAs, and services contracts into something safe to sign, and the actual judgment work gets buried under reading and re-reading boilerplate.

Danielle is an AI contract assistant that compresses that loop. You CC `danielle@danielle.legal` on an email with a contract attached, and within minutes you get back a redlined draft with inline suggestions and risk commentary, flagging things like broad liability carve-outs, vague payment terms, or clauses that violate your company's playbook. No new portal, no plugin, no workflow change.
I contributed to the Danielle codebase. This page exists to point at the live product and explain what it does, the work itself lives in the team's repos.

points.title: Email is the interface
points.desc: No tool to learn. Forward or CC a contract the way you already do, and the redline lands back in your inbox. The workflow your team already runs becomes the workflow.

points.title: Minutes, not days
points.desc: Danielle claims up to an 80% reduction in review cycle time. The first pass, the part that's mostly pattern-matching against playbooks and prior deals, stops being the bottleneck.

points.title: Your playbook, enforced
points.desc: The assistant reviews against your company's positions, not a generic checklist. Fallback language, hard-stops, and risk thresholds get applied consistently across every contract, every reviewer.

howItWorks.label: How it works
howItWorks.title: From inbox to redline
howItWorks.subtitle: Three steps, zero new software for the user.

steps.title: Send the contract
steps.desc: CC `danielle@danielle.legal` on an email with the contract attached, DOCX or PDF. No upload portal, no integration to install.

steps.title: AI review against your playbook
steps.desc: Danielle parses the document, runs clause-level analysis against your company's positions and risk tolerances, and generates tracked-changes plus inline comments explaining each suggestion.

steps.title: Redline back in your inbox
steps.desc: Minutes later you get a marked-up draft with rationale. Counsel does the judgment work, accept, reject, negotiate, instead of the reading work.

tech.label: Under the hood
tech.title: What the product is built on
tech.subtitle: A modern web app over an email-ingestion + LLM analysis pipeline.

items: Next.js (App Router) with React Server Components
TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui on Radix primitives
lucide-react icons, Inter via `next/font`, Calendly embed for demo booking
Email-ingestion pipeline as the primary user interface
DOCX parsing and tracked-changes generation for redlined output
LLM-driven clause analysis tuned to company-specific playbooks
